title: Improve Daily Sales Report & Add Product Management

requirements.text: Extend the backend getZReport (and any daily sales query) to return, for each product sold today: product name, quantity sold, selling price, purchase price (last known buy price), revenue (quantity × selling price), and profit (quantity × (selling price - purchase price)). Also return totals: total daily revenue, total daily profit, and total quantity sold across all products.
acceptanceCriteria: getZReport returns per-product rows each containing: productName, quantitySold, sellingPrice, purchasePrice, revenue, profit
getZReport returns aggregate fields: totalRevenue, totalProfit, totalQuantitySold
Profit per product is calculated as quantity × (sellingPrice - purchasePrice)
The last known purchase price for each product is read from the products stable map

requirements.text: Update DailySalesReport.tsx to display the enriched report data returned by getZReport. For each product sold today, show: product name, quantity sold, selling price (AZN), purchase price (AZN), revenue (AZN), and profit (AZN). Below the per-product list, show summary totals: Total Quantity Sold, Total Daily Revenue, and Total Daily Profit. All labels in English.
acceptanceCriteria: Each product row shows: name, qty sold, selling price, buy price, revenue, profit
A summary section at the bottom shows total quantity sold, total revenue, total profit
All monetary values are formatted to 2 decimal places with AZN suffix
If no sales exist for today, a message like 'No sales recorded today' is shown

requirements.text: Add backend functions for product management: (1) addProduct(name: Text) — adds a new product with zero stock and zero purchase price to the stable products map, returning an error if the name already exists; (2) deleteProduct(name: Text) — removes the product from the stable products map and removes all associated sale records from the stable sales list, returning an error if the product does not exist.

quotes: Added products must appear permanently in menus
Deleted products must be removed from inventory and menus

acceptanceCriteria: addProduct accepts a product name string and persists it in stable storage with quantity=0 and purchasePrice=0
addProduct returns an error variant if the product name already exists
deleteProduct removes the product entry from the stable products map
deleteProduct also removes all sale records for that product from the stable sales list
deleteProduct returns an error variant if the product name is not found
getStockLevels and getZReport reflect added/deleted products immediately after the call

requirements.text: Add React Query mutation hooks useAddProduct and useDeleteProduct in useQueries.ts that call the backend addProduct and deleteProduct functions respectively. On success, invalidate all relevant queries (stock levels, sales history, Z-report).
acceptanceCriteria: useAddProduct mutation calls actor.addProduct(name) and invalidates stock and sales queries on success
useDeleteProduct mutation calls actor.deleteProduct(name) and invalidates stock and sales queries on success
Both hooks expose isPending, isError, and error states for UI feedback

requirements.text: Create a new Product Management page (ProductManagement.tsx) accessible from the Dashboard via a new 'Manage Products' button. The page must be password-protected (password: 'gulserxan'). After authentication, display: (1) a text input field and an 'Add Product' button to add a new product by name; (2) a list of all current products (fetched via getStockLevels) each with a delete (trash) button. Confirm deletion with an inline confirmation prompt before calling deleteProduct. Show success and error feedback for both add and delete actions. All labels in English.

quotes: Added products must appear permanently in menus
Deleted products must be removed from inventory and menus

acceptanceCriteria: Page is reachable from the Dashboard via a 'Manage Products' button
Page shows a PasswordGate before revealing content
After authentication, a text input and 'Add Product' button are visible
Submitting a valid product name calls useAddProduct and shows a success message
Each product in the list has a delete button; tapping it shows an inline confirmation
Confirming deletion calls useDeleteProduct and the product disappears from the list
Error messages are shown if add or delete fails (e.g., duplicate name, product not found)
The product list is fetched from getStockLevels and refreshes after add/delete

requirements.text: Update the Dashboard (Dashboard.tsx) to include a new 'Manage Products' navigation button that routes to the ProductManagement page. The button must follow the same full-width, large, mobile-first styling as existing dashboard buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard renders a 'Manage Products' button alongside existing action buttons
Tapping the button navigates to the ProductManagement page
Button matches the styling (size, padding, rounded corners) of other dashboard buttons

requirements.text: Update App.tsx to register a route for the ProductManagement page (e.g., /product-management).
acceptanceCriteria: A route for /product-management (or equivalent) is defined in the TanStack Router config
The route renders the ProductManagement component
The back button in the header navigates back to the Dashboard from this page

requirements.text: Ensure the AddStock, RecordSale, ViewInventory, SalesHistory, and DailySalesReport pages dynamically fetch the current product list (via getStockLevels) and render product selection buttons for all products currently in stable storage — including newly added products and excluding deleted ones. Remove any hardcoded product name arrays from these pages.

quotes: Added products must appear permanently in menus
Deleted products must be removed from inventory and menus

acceptanceCriteria: AddStock product selection step shows buttons for all products returned by getStockLevels
RecordSale product selection step shows buttons for all products returned by getStockLevels
ViewInventory list shows all products returned by getStockLevels
SalesHistory product filter includes all products returned by getStockLevels
No page references a hardcoded static array of product names
After a product is added or deleted via ProductManagement, the menus reflect the change on next data fetch

requirements.text: Generate a migration module (migration.mo) that preserves all existing stable data (products map and sales list) when upgrading the canister to the new version that adds the addProduct and deleteProduct functions.

quotes: Added products must appear permanently in menus

acceptanceCriteria: migration.mo defines OldActor with the previous stable state shape
migration.mo defines NewActor with the updated stable state shape
The run function copies products and sales from old to new state without data loss
No existing stock quantities or sale records are lost after canister upgrade

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Monetary values must be formatted to 2 decimal places
nonGoals: Changing the existing password or authentication mechanism
Adding server-side authentication or Internet Identity for product management
Modifying the WhatsApp Z-report sharing format beyond the new profit/revenue fields
